title: Samlor Korko (Cambodian Stirring Pot Soup)
description: A cornerstone of Cambodian home cooking, Samlor Korko is a complex, nutrient-dense soup that tells the story of the Khmer countryside. It's a 'stirring pot' of seasonal vegetables, balanced with the pungent depth of prahok and the aromatic citrus pull of hand-ground kroeung.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Pork belly or shoulder","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"cut into bite-sized pieces"},{"name":"Yellow Kroeung paste","qty":"3","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, garlic, shallots"},{"name":"Prahok (Fermented fish paste)","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"minced into a fine paste"},{"name":"Pumpkin","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"peeled and cubed"},{"name":"Green Papaya","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"shredded or cubed"},{"name":"Roasted Rice Powder","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"toasted until nutty brown"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Craft the Kroeung**: If not using pre-made, finely dice the lemongrass and galangal. Pound them in a stone mortar with fresh turmeric and kaffir lime leaves until a bright yellow, fragrant paste forms. Set aside.
2. **Prahok Preparation**: Mince the prahok into a fine paste. In a large heavy-bottomed pot, sear the pork over medium heat with the kroeung and prahok. Stir continuously until the meat is fully coated and the aromas 'bloom'—you'll smell a rich, toasted citrus scent.
3. **Vegetable Layering**: Add the pumpkin and green papaya first. Stir for 3-4 minutes, allowing them to absorb the spice base. This 'stirring' step is traditional and builds deep flavor.
4. **Simmer and Finish**: Pour in 1 liter of water. Bring to a gentle boil, then simmer until the vegetables are tender but not mushy. At the very last minute, stir in the roasted rice powder to thicken the broth. Season with a pinch of palm sugar for balance.
imagePrompt: Close-up, high-end food photography of Samlor Korko soup in a rustic ceramic bowl. Visible chunks of pumpkin, green papaya, and tender pork. The broth is thick and golden-green from kroeung. Garnish of fresh holy basil. Soft shadows, warm ambient lighting, 8k resolution, professional culinary plating.
cookwareJson: ["Stone mortar and pestle", "Large heavy-bottomed pot", "Sharp knife", "Wooden stirring spoon"]

title: Khao Soi (Northern Thai Coconut Curry Noodle Soup)
description: A soul-warming masterpiece from Northern Thailand, Khao Soi is a symphony of textures and temperatures. Silky egg noodles bathed in a rich, coconut-milk-infused curry broth, crowned with a nest of golden crispy noodles and brightened with pickled mustard greens and lime.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Chicken thighs (bone-in)","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"skin-on for better fat rendering"},{"name":"Red curry paste","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"high quality Mae Ploy or similar"},{"name":"Coconut milk (full fat)","qty":"400","unit":"ml","notes":"use first-press for maximum richness"},{"name":"Egg noodles","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"thin, fresh noodles preferred"},{"name":"Turmeric and Ginger","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"finely grated"},{"name":"Pickled Mustard Greens","qty":"30","unit":"g","notes":"rinsed and sliced for garnish"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Develop the Paste base**: In a heavy skillet or Dutch oven, sear the chicken thighs skin-side down until the fat renders and the skin is golden-crisp. Remove the chicken. In the same fat, fry the red curry paste with grated turmeric and ginger until the oil 'splits'—a sign that the spices are fully toasted.
2. **Infuse the Broth**: Add half the coconut milk and whisk into a smooth, bubbling base. Return the chicken to the pot and add the remaining milk. Simmer on low heat for 25 minutes until the chicken is tender enough to fall off the bone.
3. **The Texture Play (Noodles)**: Boil the egg noodles until al dente. Drain and set aside. Take a small handful of raw noodles and flash-fry them in hot oil for 30 seconds until they puff into golden, crunchy ribbons.
4. **The Plating Ritual**: Place the soft noodles in a deep, pre-warmed bowl. Ladle the rich curry and a whole chicken thigh over them. Crown the dish with the crispy noodle nest. Arrange shallots, pickled greens, and lime on a side plate to be added by the diner for a perfect acidic balance.
imagePrompt: Vibrant overhead shot of Khao Soi noodle soup in a blue patterned Thai bowl. Dark, rich orange coconut curry broth. A nest of bright golden crispy noodles sits on top. Side dish of purple shallots, green pickled mustard greens, and a lime wedge. Dramatic side lighting, steam rising, high contrast, culinary magazine style.
cookwareJson: ["Heavy skillet or Dutch oven", "Large pot for noodles", "Small frying pan", "Whisk or wooden spoon"]

title: Bánh Cuốn (Vietnamese Steamed Rice Rolls)
description: A testament to Vietnamese culinary precision, Bánh Cuốn features translucent, paper-thin rice sheets steamed to ethereal lightness. Filled with savory minced pork and earthy wood ear mushrooms, these rolls are a morning ritual, served with crisp shallots and the sharp, floral notes of a perfectly balanced nuoc cham.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Rice flour and Tapioca starch","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"3:1 ratio for perfect elasticity"},{"name":"Ground pork","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"finely minced"},{"name":"Wood ear mushrooms","qty":"30","unit":"g","notes":"rehydrated and minced"},{"name":"Crispy Shallots","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"house-fried for maximum crunch"},{"name":"Nuoc Cham (Dipping sauce)","qty":"50","unit":"ml","notes":"Fish sauce, lime, garlic, chili, sugar"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Prepare the Filling**: Finely mince the wood ear mushrooms until they resemble dark sand. Sauté with ground pork and a heavy pinch of white pepper over high heat until the pork is fully cooked and the mushrooms are tender and aromatic.
2. **Mix the Batter**: Whisk the rice flour and tapioca starch with water and a tablespoon of oil. Let the batter rest for at least 30 minutes—this is crucial for hydrating the starches to ensure the rolls are both supple and strong.
3. **Steam the Sheets**: Using a traditional steamer or a pristine non-stick pan, pour a thin ladle of batter and spread it into a paper-thin circle. Cover for 30 seconds. Lift the delicate sheet with a bamboo stick using a steady, rhythmic motion.
4. **Roll and Garnish**: Place a small spoonful of the pork-mushroom mixture on the edge of the sheet and roll it into a neat, translucent cylinder. Top with a mount of crispy shallots and serve immediately with blanched bean sprouts and herbs for a fresh, textural contrast.
cookwareJson: ["Traditional steamer or non-stick pan", "Small skillet", "Bamboo stick or offset spatula", "Whisk"]

title: Cá Kho Tộ (Vietnamese Caramelized Catfish)
description: A cornerstone of Vietnamese home cooking, Cá Kho Tộ is a soulful, clay-pot-braised dish that masterfully balances savory, sweet, and smoky notes. The catfish develops a deep amber glaze in a caramel sauce, accented by peppercorns and pork fat for a velvety, unctuous finish.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Catfish steaks","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"thick-cut for better braising"},{"name":"Palm sugar","qty":"3","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for the caramel base"},{"name":"Coconut water","qty":"200","unit":"ml","notes":"fresh is best for natural sweetness"},{"name":"Fish sauce","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"premium quality (e.g., Red Boat)"},{"name":"Black Peppercorns","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"freshly cracked for heat"},{"name":"Shallots and Garlic","qty":"3","unit":"pcs","notes":"minced and sautéed"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Craft the Caramel base**: In a heavy clay pot or high-sided skillet, melt the palm sugar with a dash of water over medium-high heat. Watch carefully until it reaches a deep, mahogany amber. This 'nuoc mau' is the essential flavor foundation.
2. **Sear and Color**: Quickly sear the catfish steaks in the hot caramel on both sides until they absorb the dark, rich color. This step locks in the moisture and allows the caramel to penetrate the fish core.
3. **The Braise**: Pour in the fresh coconut water and fish sauce. The coconut water will deglaze the pot and create a glossy, balanced sauce. Add the minced shallots, garlic, and whole chilies.
4. **The Reduction**: Lower the heat and simmer gently for 15-20 minutes, basting the fish occasionally. The sauce should reduce until it's thick and syrupy, clinging to the catfish like a second skin. Finish with a heavy crack of black pepper and fresh scallions.
cookwareJson: ["Clay pot (traditional) or heavy skillet", "Small saucepan", "Tongs", "Mortar and pestle for pepper"]

title: Pleah Sach Ko (Cambodian Beef Ceviche)
description: Pleah Sach Ko is a vibrant, celebratory salad that captures the essence of Cambodian freshness. Thinly sliced prime beef is 'cooked' in the bright acidity of lime juice, then tossed with an aromatic garden of herbs, crunchy bird's eye chilies, and the savory, salted depth of prahok.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Beef tenderloin or sirloin","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"chilled for easier thin slicing"},{"name":"Lime juice","qty":"100","unit":"ml","notes":"freshly squeezed for the 'cooking' process"},{"name":"Lemongrass","qty":"2","unit":"stalks","notes":"very finely slivered"},{"name":"Prahok (minced)","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"provides the essential umami depth"},{"name":"Fresh Herbs (Cilantro, Sawtooth, Mint)","qty":"1","unit":"bunch","notes":"roughly chopped for freshness"},{"name":"Roasted Peanuts","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"crushed for a crunchy finish"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Acid-Cooking the Beef**: Slice the chilled beef into paper-thin, translucent ribbons. Place them in a ceramic bowl and drench with fresh lime juice. Let the beef 'cook' for 15-20 minutes until the edges turn opaque. Gently squeeze out any excess liquid to keep the salad crisp.
2. **Aromatic Infusion**: In a pestle, pound the slivered lemongrass with bird's eye chilies and garlic into a vibrant, zesty paste. Whisk in the minced prahok and a spoonful of palm sugar for a balanced Khmer dressing.
3. **Tossing the Garden**: Combine the 'cooked' beef with the aromatic dressing and a large handful of cilantro, sawtooth herb, and kaffir lime slivers. The contrast between the tender beef and the garden-fresh herbs is the dish's hallmark.
4. **Final Flourish**: Plate the salad on a large banana leaf or a bright serving dish. Sprinkle with a generous amount of crushed roasted peanuts and crispy fried shallots for a nutty, crunchy finish that counteracts the lime's acidity.
cookwareJson: ["Stone mortar and pestle", "Large ceramic mixing bowl", "Sharp chef knife", "Serving platter (or banana leaf)"]
